channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Pro-worker AI,' streaming fatalities, and other fascinating new economic studies
item.description: From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.
item.content:encoded:
From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.

(Image credit: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Consumers concerned after Iran war drives gas prices higher
item.description: Gas prices remain higher than usual across the US, as the price of crude oil has been swinging wildly in response to the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices remain higher than usual across the US, as the price of crude oil has been swinging wildly in response to the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Iran war bottlenecks vital waterway raising fears of a global energy crisis
item.description: War chokes the Strait of Hormuz, leaving hundreds of oil tankers and container ships stranded and raising alarms of a looming global energy shock and food shortages across the Gulf and beyond.
item.content:encoded: War chokes the Strait of Hormuz, leaving hundreds of oil tankers and container ships stranded and raising alarms of a looming global energy shock and food shortages across the Gulf and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Trump set a target of 1 million apprenticeships. Here's how that's going
item.description: President Trump has embraced apprenticeships as a solution for workforce needs. But many doubt the money invested so far will be enough to reach Trump's goal of 1 million apprenticeships.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has embraced apprenticeships as a solution for workforce needs. But many doubt the money invested so far will be enough to reach Trump's goal of 1 million apprenticeships.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.
item.content:encoded:
Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: No lawsuits required: U.S. Customs is working on a system to refund tariffs
item.description: U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The U.S. unexpectedly loses 92,000 jobs, adding to worries about the economy
item.description: The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.
item.content:encoded:
The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Wall Street is betting on tariff refunds after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.
item.content:encoded:
When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Vito Emanuel

item.title: Gas prices jump and stocks plunge as fallout from the war with Iran spreads
item.description: Oil prices are up and stock prices are down, as financial markets react to the U.S. war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Oil prices are up and stock prices are down, as financial markets react to the U.S. war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: U.S. gasoline prices set to jump with Iran war
item.description: The U.S. war with Iran is rattling energy and financial markets. Gasoline prices jumped overnight while stock prices tumbled.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. war with Iran is rattling energy and financial markets. Gasoline prices jumped overnight while stock prices tumbled.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
